response.justification: Wei Chen is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong performance optimization and system design skills, but this role requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, and AI accelerator hardware-software co-design. The candidate's background is entirely in software development (web, distributed systems, DevOps) with no evidence of hardware design, hardware description languages, or accelerator architecture experience. While educational and seniority requirements are met, the fundamental technical domain is misaligned.
strengths: Exceptional software engineer with 14+ years experience and proven ability to optimize performance-critical systems at scale (micro-services handling millions of requests/sec, low-latency applications)
Demonstrated system-level thinking through infrastructure work, platform architecture, and infrastructure automation with DevOps, Docker, and Kubernetes
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure through current role at re:cinq with AI-agent systems and MCP frameworks, showing awareness of emerging domains
concerns: No experience with hardware description languages, SoC modeling, IP integration, or any hardware design fundamentals required for the core responsibilities
Lacks specialized knowledge in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, and deep learning workload optimization essential for principal-level accelerator work
Background is exclusively in software engineering (web, distributed systems, DevOps) with no evidence of hardware-software co-design or accelerator-specific performance analysis
